buyer_views.question: Which agentic AI risks are critical or high, who owns them, what SLA applies, and which controls should be implemented first?
buyer_views.title: Security Prioritization

buyer_views.question: What deterministic decision should a gateway or agent host return when a runtime event matches one of the scored agentic risk scenarios?
buyer_views.title: AI Platform Runtime Gate

buyer_views.question: Does SecurityRecipes translate emerging agentic-security standards into a productizable scoring layer with evidence, MCP tools, and hosted expansion wedges?
buyer_views.title: Acquisition Diligence

commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI coding platform, or security vendor gets a quantitative severity layer that can attach to every agent action, MCP tool, and trust-center export.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Sell hosted AIVSS drift scoring with live MCP gateway logs, skill registries, Agent Cards, approval receipts, and customer-private context sources.
commercialization_path.open_layer: Publish a deterministic AIVSS-aligned risk score for the open secure-context and MCP evidence packs.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Let teams fork the profile, tune scenario vectors, and bind the score to their own agent identities, MCP servers, tools, and telemetry.

decision_contract.basis: AIVSS-aligned severity rubric for agentic AI and MCP risks. This profile uses an explicit 0-10 scoring model so CI and MCP tools can reproduce the result; it does not claim to be the official OWASP AIVSS calculator.

dimensions.question: How much can the agent plan or continue without direct human review?
dimensions.title: Autonomy Exposure

dimensions.question: Can the agent use delegated identity, privileged tools, external writes, production actions, or irreversible operations?
dimensions.title: Authority and Impact

dimensions.question: Can poisoned, stale, private, regulated, or tenant-sensitive context influence the action?
dimensions.title: Context and Data Sensitivity

dimensions.question: Can MCP tools, A2A handoffs, browser workspaces, skills, or remote agent surfaces amplify the issue?
dimensions.title: Tool and Protocol Reachability

dimensions.question: Is the failure mode already represented in current OWASP, incident, red-team, or supply-chain evidence?
dimensions.title: Exploit Maturity

dimensions.question: How much confidence remains after current source-controlled controls, telemetry, approvals, and generated evidence are considered?
dimensions.title: Control Confidence

dimensions.question: Would this risk materially affect procurement, platform adoption, design-partner trust, or acquisition diligence?
dimensions.title: Business Materiality

decision_contract.model_name: SecurityRecipes Agentic AIVSS Risk Scoring v1

score_bands.remediation_sla: 24h owner triage; block production expansion until compensating controls or explicit risk acceptance exist
7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
30d remediation or documented acceptance; monitor with telemetry and run receipts
track in posture backlog; review when scope, autonomy, identity, or tool reach changes

hosted_mcp_wedges.wedge: MCP session risk policy that denies dangerous tool combinations before a gateway forwards calls to enterprise systems.
Shadow-agent and shadow-MCP discovery intake with governance workflow and trust-center export.
Runtime action firewall for code execution, STDIO launch, package-manager, and local developer-tool boundaries.
Agent identity entitlement review and authorization PDP for MCP gateways and agent hosts.
Real-time context poisoning score, source demotion, and retrieval kill decisions before private context reaches an agent.
Skill, tool, and MCP server registry scanner with signed baselines and promotion gates.
A2A and agent-handoff trust monitor with signed Agent Card drift detection.
Agentic runtime telemetry, runaway-loop detection, and incident replay export.
Approval receipt verification and two-key review enforcement before privileged agent actions.

intent: Turn current agentic AI and MCP security guidance into a repeatable severity, SLA, and runtime-decision layer for SecurityRecipes. The profile gives enterprise, platform, product-security, and acquisition reviewers a quantitative view of agentic risk without pretending that open reference evidence is the same as live customer telemetry.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, AppSec, IAM, GRC, AI governance, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI risk scoring and remediation prioritization for secure context and MCP operations
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can score agentic AI risks with source-backed severity, map each score to generated evidence, route runtime events to allow/hold/deny/kill decisions, and show which hosted MCP controls would turn reference guidance into production enforcement.
positioning.why_now: Agentic applications now combine autonomy, delegated identity, external tools, persistent memory, inter-agent handoffs, and MCP servers. OWASP AIVSS, the OWASP Agentic Top 10, OWASP MCP Top 10, and Agentic Skills guidance create a shared language, but buyers still need severity, ownership, SLA, and proof surfaces that can be consumed through MCP.

remediation_queue.first_control: Split sessions that combine private data, untrusted content, and exfiltration-capable write tools.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 24h owner triage; block production expansion until compensating controls or explicit risk acceptance exist
remediation_queue.title: MCP Tool Misuse in a Lethal Session Combination

remediation_queue.first_control: Require app intake for every agent host, MCP server, and connector before production use.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Rogue Agent or Shadow MCP Server

remediation_queue.first_control: Keep STDIO launch commands exact and sandboxed.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Unexpected Code Execution Through Tool or Skill Paths

remediation_queue.first_control: Use per-workflow non-human identities with explicit denies.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Identity and Privilege Abuse by an Agent

remediation_queue.first_control: Demote retrieved context from instruction to cited evidence.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Agent Goal Hijack Through Poisoned Context

remediation_queue.first_control: Pin skill, tool, and Agent Card hashes before execution.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Agentic Supply Chain or Skill Compromise

remediation_queue.first_control: Treat Agent Cards and handoff metadata as untrusted until signed, pinned, and policy-mapped.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Insecure Inter-Agent Handoff or Remote Agent Trust

remediation_queue.first_control: Define per-run budgets, loop limits, fan-out limits, and kill conditions.
remediation_queue.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
remediation_queue.title: Cascading Failure or Runaway Agent Operation

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: MCP session risk policy that denies dangerous tool combinations before a gateway forwards calls to enterprise systems.
recommended_controls: Split sessions that combine private data, untrusted content, and exfiltration-capable write tools.
Require tool annotations, namespace allowlists, and scope challenge handling.
Block capability expansion until tool-surface drift is reviewed.
Emit MCP decision telemetry for every high-impact tool call.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 24h owner triage; block production expansion until compensating controls or explicit risk acceptance exist
risk_scores.title: MCP Tool Misuse in a Lethal Session Combination

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Shadow-agent and shadow-MCP discovery intake with governance workflow and trust-center export.
recommended_controls: Require app intake for every agent host, MCP server, and connector before production use.
Deny unregistered agents and shadow MCP servers by default.
Tie agent identities, scopes, connectors, and owner teams to posture evidence.
Expose inventory and exceptions through the enterprise trust-center export.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Rogue Agent or Shadow MCP Server

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Runtime action firewall for code execution, STDIO launch, package-manager, and local developer-tool boundaries.
recommended_controls: Keep STDIO launch commands exact and sandboxed.
Deny dynamic shell, interpreter, package-manager, and network expansion without approval.
Require action-runtime decisions before code, deploy, registry, or filesystem actions.
Use kill signals for command injection, malicious package, or untrusted config execution.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Unexpected Code Execution Through Tool or Skill Paths

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Agent identity entitlement review and authorization PDP for MCP gateways and agent hosts.
recommended_controls: Use per-workflow non-human identities with explicit denies.
Bind tokens to resource audiences and short-lived permission leases.
Require step-up approvals for privilege expansion.
Revoke identity sessions when the workflow, user, or connector drifts.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Identity and Privilege Abuse by an Agent

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Real-time context poisoning score, source demotion, and retrieval kill decisions before private context reaches an agent.
recommended_controls: Demote retrieved context from instruction to cited evidence.
Require poisoning scans and source hashes before retrieval.
Hold high-autonomy actions when untrusted context influences tool selection.
Bind every answer and tool decision to a run receipt.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Agent Goal Hijack Through Poisoned Context

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Skill, tool, and MCP server registry scanner with signed baselines and promotion gates.
recommended_controls: Pin skill, tool, and Agent Card hashes before execution.
Verify publisher, registry, sandbox, and network restrictions.
Deny untrusted skill updates that request shell, network, credential, or filesystem expansion.
Route shadow MCP servers to intake before promotion.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Agentic Supply Chain or Skill Compromise

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: A2A and agent-handoff trust monitor with signed Agent Card drift detection.
recommended_controls: Treat Agent Cards and handoff metadata as untrusted until signed, pinned, and policy-mapped.
Bound handoff payload fields and data classes before context crosses agents.
Require target-agent authorization, observability, and receipt binding.
Deny opaque remote agents requesting high-impact tools or memory writes.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Insecure Inter-Agent Handoff or Remote Agent Trust

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Agentic runtime telemetry, runaway-loop detection, and incident replay export.
recommended_controls: Define per-run budgets, loop limits, fan-out limits, and kill conditions.
Emit telemetry for every model, tool, policy, egress, approval, and verifier event.
Bind incidents to replayable traces and receipts.
Require containment decisions before an agent resumes after a severe signal.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 7d remediation plan; guarded pilot only with receipts, owner review, and rollback
risk_scores.title: Cascading Failure or Runaway Agent Operation

risk_scores.hosted_mcp_wedge: Approval receipt verification and two-key review enforcement before privileged agent actions.
recommended_controls: Make approvals scoped, time-boxed, auditable, and bound to the exact action envelope.
Deny approval reuse after context, tool, risk, or output changes.
Separate reviewer roles for high-impact or irreversible operations.
Run red-team drills for approval bypass, social-engineered prompts, and hidden instruction chains.
risk_scores.remediation_sla: 30d remediation or documented acceptance; monitor with telemetry and run receipts
risk_scores.title: Human-Agent Trust Exploitation or Approval Bypass

source_references.name: OWASP AI Vulnerability Scoring System v0.8
source_references.why_it_matters: AIVSS is the most directly relevant emerging attempt to score agentic AI security risk rather than treating all AI failures as generic application vulnerabilities.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications
source_references.why_it_matters: The Agentic Top 10 names the practical risk families that matter when agents can pursue goals, chain tools, use delegated identity, and coordinate across systems.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP security risks such as token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, authorization failure, telemetry gaps, shadow servers, and context over-sharing define the protocol layer this product secures.

source_references.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: Agent skills are the behavior and execution layer between model intent and tool calls, so severity scoring must account for skill provenance, privilege, isolation, update drift, and registry compromise.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST AI 600-1 provides the governance, mapping, measurement, and management framing enterprise buyers expect when AI risk scores become operational decisions.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: The MCP authorization specification anchors resource indicators, audience binding, token handling, and scope minimization, which are decisive controls for scoring delegated tool authority.
